action_metadata.metadata_title: Alchemy by Sundial x Charms: Cardano-Native Bitcoin Treasury Protocol

onchain_context:
onchain recipient history: stake address stake17xzc8pt7fgf0lc0x7eq6z7z6puhsxmzktna7dluahrj6g6ghh5qjr appears in 61 withdrawal proposals totalling 660216978000000 lovelace (first 2025-07-17 02:35:25, last 2026-06-24 03:01:28)

fact_verdicts.fact: Action id is 0f75dd11be0b7a6cb4b305a175b17cf4b60cd307c052fccabf9f572950e70583#0; action type is TreasuryWithdrawals; proposed epoch is 639; expires after epoch 646.
fact_verdicts.note: Source Material 0 states all four values exactly.

fact_verdicts.fact: The supplied action header lists treasury amount (lovelace) as '(none)'.
fact_verdicts.note: Source Material 0 lists Treasury amount (lovelace): (none).

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor title is 'Alchemy by Sundial x Charms: Cardano-Native Bitcoin Treasury Protocol'.
fact_verdicts.note: Source Material 3 body.title matches exactly.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states the proposal requests 10,000,000 ada, using a reference value of approximately USD 0.20 per ada, for a total planning budget of approximately USD 2.0 million.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor abstract and rationale state these figures.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor splits the request into two separated pools: Pool 1 approximately USD 1.0 million for protocol infrastructure and staged launch liquidity; Pool 2 approximately USD 1.0 million for delivery, audit, integrations, dashboards, governance reporting, legal/compliance work, and go-to-market execution.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor abstract states this split and purposes.

fact_verdicts.fact: Pool 1 deployment is staged over three months: Month 1 approximately USD 250,000 after audit and launch-readiness review; Month 2 approximately USD 250,000 after public reporting, operational review, and a 30-day grace period; Month 3 approximately USD 500,000 after mint/redeem thresholds, reserve-ratio tracking, growth-rate monitoring, and dashboard performance are confirmed.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor rationale states the three-month staged deployment with these tranche amounts and conditions.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states the initial reserve target is approximately USD 800,000 FIRE-side liquidity and approximately USD 200,000 ICE-side liquidity, subject to final optimization based on economic modeling, audit feedback, and launch conditions.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor rationale states this exactly.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states each tranche is expected to be purchased at an approximate 4:1 FIRE-to-ICE ratio to move the system toward a 5.0x reserve ratio.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor rationale states this exactly.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states launch liquidity will be treasury-supported and treasury-owned, and all profits, yield, and returns generated by the launch liquidity position will be returned to the Cardano Treasury quarterly, converted into ada through Cardano-native DEXs at commercially reasonable rates, with conversion timing and methodology disclosed in monthly reports.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor rationale states these launch-liquidity terms.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states launch-liquidity principal can be returned to the Treasury after Alchemy reaches a 30-day time-weighted average TVL of at least USD 60 million, subject to a formal governance proposal for DRep and Constitutional Committee review.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor rationale states this condition and review path.

fact_verdicts.fact: Pool 2 planned allocation is: USD 250,000 protocol infrastructure; USD 300,000 engineering, platform, delivery, monitoring, reporting, and capital-formation support; USD 200,000 independent security review, audit, and economic modeling; USD 100,000 public dashboards, wallet flows, DEX adapters, documentation, and composability guides; USD 75,000 legal, compliance, disclosures, treasury reporting, risk documentation, and launch-readiness review; USD 50,000 ecosystem education, DRep communications, partner activation, and launch coordination; USD 25,000 fund administration and accounting.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor rationale gives this itemized Pool 2 allocation.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states funds may be reallocated between delivery categories as requirements are finalized, but total delivery spend may not exceed the approved delivery budget; material reallocations will be disclosed.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor rationale states this.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states the infrastructure and launch liquidity pool remains separate and cannot be used for implementation overruns, general operating expenses, personal compensation, or any purpose outside approved deployment.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor rationale states this restriction.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states there are no DRep funds or personal compensation to Sundial or Charms principals.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor rationale states this directly.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states that if Sundial or Charms receives external investment for Alchemy development, the development portion of the treasury request will roll into additional treasury-supported launch liquidity instead of duplicating compensation.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor abstract and rationale state this rollover commitment.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states maximum acceptable ADA reference rate is USD 0.35; if ADA appreciates above that level before or during delivery, future milestone withdrawals will be reduced or excess ada will be returned.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor rationale states this ADA price protection.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states deployment pauses if milestones are missed, reporting lapses, liquidity-health thresholds are breached, or material risks emerge, until the issue is resolved or governance provides direction.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor rationale states these pause rules.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states unused delivery funds, excess ada resulting from price protection, and profits/yield/returns generated by launch liquidity will be returned to the Treasury under the reporting process.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor rationale states these refund and return conditions.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor proposes Intersect as interim fund administrator, subject to confirmation and final agreement; if Intersect does not confirm within 30 days of enactment, the proposer will nominate an alternative independent administrator, subject to community notice and appropriate constitutional review.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor rationale states this administrator fallback.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states any ada held by the administrator will be kept in auditable accounts and delegated to auto-abstain, not to a Stake Pool Operator.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor abstract and rationale state this.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states Sundial remains accountable for controlled deployment of treasury-linked funds, compliance with use restrictions, reporting, coordination with Charms, and delivery of the funded scope.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor rationale states Sundial's accountability in these terms.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states Sundial will disclose related-party relationships and material commercial arrangements involving Sundial, Charms, or third parties connected to this proposal.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor rationale states this disclosure commitment.

fact_verdicts.fact: The proposed Alchemy mechanics include a shared BTC reserve architecture, FIRE and ICE backed by the same BTC reserve, reserve ratio formula (V x P) / L, FIRE price formula ((V x P) - L) / N+, and an initial target reserve ratio of 5.0x.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor rationale states each of these mechanics.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor describes safety zones: above 4.0x normal FIRE and ICE minting and redemption are enabled and ICE growth continues under formula; between 2.0x and 4.0x new ICE minting and FIRE redemption are constrained while incentives are dynamically balanced; below 2.0x FIRE and ICE minting and redemption are constrained and new risky activity pauses.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor rationale describes these safety zones.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor identifies main risks as BTC volatility, oracle risk, bridge risk, Charms protocol-layer risk, novel asset user-understanding and regulatory-treatment risk, delivery risk, and adoption risk.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor rationale's risk paragraph identifies these risks.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor names mitigation measures including 5.0x target reserve ratio, hard safety zones, staged deployment, independent security review, economic modeling, dashboards, pause rules, legal/compliance work, disclosures, milestone gating, fund separation, reporting, administrator oversight, launch liquidity, integrations, go-to-market execution, and rollover commitment.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor rationale names these mitigation measures across the risk and protections sections.

fact_verdicts.fact: On-chain recipient history states stake address stake17xzc8pt7fgf0lc0x7eq6z7z6puhsxmzktna7dluahrj6g6ghh5qjr appears in 61 withdrawal proposals totalling 660216978000000 lovelace, first 2025-07-17 02:35:25 and last 2026-06-24 03:01:28.
fact_verdicts.note: Source Material 1 states this recipient history exactly.

fact_verdicts.fact: On-chain DRep vote counts so far are yes=14 no=25 abstain=9, explicit ballots and not stake-weighted.
fact_verdicts.note: Source Material 1 states these vote counts and qualifier.

fact_verdicts.fact: Treasury flow over 36 epochs is inflow 158838297854652 lovelace, enacted withdrawals 361435336000000 lovelace, ratio 2.275492.
fact_verdicts.note: Source Material 1 states these treasury-flow figures exactly.

fact_verdicts.fact: BEACN doctrine prioritizes treasury stewardship and downside protection above evidence quality and reproducibility, public-benefit ecosystem growth, and execution speed/social consensus momentum, while constitutional integrity and protocol safety is highest.
fact_verdicts.note: Source Material 2 values hierarchy lists this ordering.

fact_verdicts.fact: BEACN doctrine states that if ecosystem growth conflicts with treasury stewardship and evidence is weak, the default is to prefer ABSTAIN.
fact_verdicts.note: Source Material 2 conflict handling defaults state this.

fact_verdicts.fact: BEACN doctrine states a soft gate for TreasuryWithdrawals without a complete dossier: score directionally with an explicit -0.10 caution penalty on top of base treasury penalty and conservative reasoning lean; incomplete diligence is always recorded as explicit uncertainty.
fact_verdicts.note: Source Material 2 Dossier Gate Posture states this.

fact_verdicts.fact: BEACN doctrine rejects first-come-first-served budget models and adopts a rolling-window Net Change Limit framework.
fact_verdicts.note: Source Material 2 Treasury Spending Doctrine states this core position.

fact_verdicts.fact: BEACN doctrine states that within the available budget, proposals are funded by DRep approval weight descending, not submission order.
fact_verdicts.note: Source Material 2 prioritization section states this.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor references other providers or mechanisms including Intersect as proposed administrator, alternative independent administrator if Intersect does not confirm, Cardano-native DEXs for conversion, external investment as a development-funding source, and a formal governance proposal for return of principal.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor rationale references all listed providers or mechanisms.

verification.summary: All 35 dossier facts are supported by the supplied source material. No contradicted facts or invented specific figures, names, dates, or commitments were found.
